summary: Biome Makers uses DNA sequencing and machine learning to analyze the soil microbiome, providing growers with actionable insights into soil health, disease risk, and nutrient mobilization. A case study at Vinyes Domènech winery in Spain showed that using BeCrop® test recommendations led to the disappearance of diseases like Blackfoot and powdery mildew. Another Napa Valley case study reported savings of $540 per acre by tailoring crop protection practices based on soil biological data.

summary: Biorizon Biotech, based in Spain, develops and produces biostimulants and biopesticides derived from microalgae and bacteria. Their proprietary TrieTech© technology is applied to create products that improve crop quality and yield, with specific solutions developed and tested for viticulture.

summary: Agrology provides a predictive agriculture platform with proprietary in-field sensors to track and quantify key cross-cutting metrics in real-time. Their system monitors soil carbon sequestration, soil respiration (microbial activity), water stress, and GHG fluxes, providing verifiable data for regenerative viticulture projects. They have demonstrated measurable improvements in soil health and carbon cycling in vineyard-specific trials.

summary: Arable provides an integrated in-field sensing system (the Arable Mark) that combines weather, plant, soil, and irrigation data to provide a holistic view of the vineyard. summary: SupPlant is an Israeli AgTech company that uses plant-based sensors to monitor real-time water needs, providing precise irrigation recommendations. summary: UAV-IQ specializes in augmentative biological control, using drones to release beneficial insects and mites to manage vineyard pests.
